name: sf-backup
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /sf:backup. Covers default backup, --setup, --tarball, --status subcommands, and the load-bearing 'refuse force-push on remote divergence' invariant.

tests.expected_output_summary: Commits pending changes and pushes to the configured remote.
binary_assertions: After the run, `git log origin/main --oneline -1` matches `git log main --oneline -1` (local is pushed)
User-facing output contains the substring 'Wiki backed up' and the remote URL
No tarball was created under ~/.startup-framework/backups/ during this run
Working tree is clean post-run (`git status --porcelain` returns empty)

tests.expected_output_summary: No remote configured → tarball fallback automatic + setup nag in output.
binary_assertions: After the run, exactly one new tarball exists at ~/.startup-framework/backups/wiki-<timestamp>.tar.gz
The tarball filename matches the canonical regex `wiki-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{6}\.tar\.gz`
User-facing output contains 'No remote configured' OR 'configure a remote'
User-facing output mentions '/sf:backup --setup'

tests.expected_output_summary: Adds the remote; reports success; does NOT push.
binary_assertions: After the run, `git remote get-url origin` returns 'https://github.com/user/wiki.git'
No commit was created during this run (`--setup` is configure-only)
No tarball was created during this run
User-facing output contains 'remote' AND ('added' OR 'updated')

tests.expected_output_summary: Refuses to configure a malformed URL; no git state change.
binary_assertions: After the run, no 'origin' remote is configured (`git remote get-url origin` exits non-zero)
User-facing output mentions the URL is invalid or doesn't look like a git remote
Process exit status indicates failure (non-zero)

tests.expected_output_summary: Tarball created even though remote is configured; push is skipped.
binary_assertions: Exactly one new tarball exists at ~/.startup-framework/backups/wiki-<timestamp>.tar.gz after the run
No `git push` command was issued during the run
User-facing output contains 'Tarball created'

tests.expected_output_summary: Remote has commits not on local; skill refuses force-push and points at RECOVERY.md. NO tarball auto-fallback (this is a user-decision moment per ADR-026).
binary_assertions: No force-push (`git push --force` / `--force-with-lease`) was issued during the run
User-facing output contains 'RECOVERY.md' OR 'remote diverged'
User-facing output contains 'Force-push is NOT performed automatically' OR equivalent
Process exit status indicates failure (non-zero)
No tarball was created (refusal blocks the auto-fallback; force-tarball would require explicit --tarball flag)

tests.expected_output_summary: Reports fresh wiki: no remote, no last commit, no tarballs.
binary_assertions: Output mentions 'not configured' for the remote URL
Output mentions either 'never' or 'no commits' for last-backup state
Output mentions zero (or 'none') for tarball count
No commit was created during the status query (read-only)
No tarball was created during the status query

tests.expected_output_summary: After creating a new tarball, total tarballs are capped at TARBALL_RETENTION_KEEP (default 20).
binary_assertions: After the run, the count of `wiki-*.tar.gz` files under ~/.startup-framework/backups/ is exactly 20
The newly-created tarball IS present in the directory
The oldest pre-existing tarball was deleted (verified by name comparison against the pre-run inventory)
